git: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gitxsan.
gnu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gnau.
going: verb. present participle and gerund of go
gong: noun. A percussion instrument consisting of a metal disk that emits a loud resonant sound when struck with a soft hammer.
goo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gone Dau.
goon: noun. A thug; a usually muscular henchman with little intelligence.
got: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Gothic.
gout: noun. An extremely painful inflammation of joints, especially of the big toe, caused by a metabolic defect resulting in the accumulation of
gun: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mbya Guarani.
gut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Maléku Jaíka.
into: preposition. To or towards the inside of.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
not: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomatsiguenga.
nut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nùng.
onto: preposition. Arriving upon or on top of (speaking of a physical or metaphorical movement).
tigon: noun. A cross between a male tiger and a lioness.
tog: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tonga (Malawi).
ton: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tongan.
tong: noun. An instrument or tool used for manipulating things in a fire without touching them with the hands.
too: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xicotepec de Juárez Totonac.
toon: noun. A cartoon, especially an animated television show.
unit: noun. Oneness, singularity, seen as a component of a whole number; a magnitude of one.
